00029 
00030 /*
00031  * Synchronization test code.
00032  */
00033 
00034 #include <types.h>
00035 #include <lib.h>
00036 #include <clock.h>
00037 #include <thread.h>
00038 #include <synch.h>
00039 #include <test.h>
00040 
00041 #define NSEMLOOPS     63
00042 #define NLOCKLOOPS    120
00043 #define NCVLOOPS      5
00044 #define NTHREADS      32
00045 
00046 static volatile unsigned long testval1;
00047 static volatile unsigned long testval2;
00048 static volatile unsigned long testval3;
00049 static struct semaphore *testsem;
00050 static struct lock *testlock;
00051 static struct cv *testcv;
00052 static struct semaphore *donesem;
00053 
00054 static
00055 void
00056 inititems(void)
00057 {
00058         if (testsem==NULL) {
00059                 testsem = sem_create("testsem", 2);
00060                 if (testsem == NULL) {
00061                         panic("synchtest: sem_create failed\n");
00062                 }
00063         }
00064         if (testlock==NULL) {
00065                 testlock = lock_create("testlock");
00066                 if (testlock == NULL) {
00067                         panic("synchtest: lock_create failed\n");
00068                 }
00069         }
00070         if (testcv==NULL) {
00071                 testcv = cv_create("testlock");
00072                 if (testcv == NULL) {
00073                         panic("synchtest: cv_create failed\n");
00074                 }
00075         }
00076         if (donesem==NULL) {
00077                 donesem = sem_create("donesem", 0);
00078                 if (donesem == NULL) {
00079                         panic("synchtest: sem_create failed\n");
00080                 }
00081         }
00082 }
00083 
00084 static
00085 void
00086 semtestthread(void *junk, unsigned long num)
00087 {
00088         int i;
00089         (void)junk;
00090 
00091         /*
00092          * Only one of these should print at a time.
00093          */
00094         P(testsem);
00095         kprintf("Thread %2lu: ", num);
00096         for (i=0; i<NSEMLOOPS; i++) {
00097                 kprintf("%c", (int)num+64);
00098         }
00099         kprintf("\n");
00100         V(donesem);
00101 }
00102 
00103 int
00104 semtest(int nargs, char **args)
00105 {
00106         int i, result;
00107 
00108         (void)nargs;
00109         (void)args;
00110 
00111         inititems();
00112         kprintf("Starting semaphore test...\n");
00113         kprintf("If this hangs, it's broken: ");
00114         P(testsem);
00115         P(testsem);
00116         kprintf("ok\n");
00117 
00118         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00119                 result = thread_fork("semtest", semtestthread, NULL, i, NULL);
00120                 if (result) {
00121                         panic("semtest: thread_fork failed: %s\n", 
00122                               strerror(result));
00123                 }
00124         }
00125 
00126         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00127                 V(testsem);
00128                 P(donesem);
00129         }
00130 
00131         /* so we can run it again */
00132         V(testsem);
00133         V(testsem);
00134 
00135         kprintf("Semaphore test done.\n");
00136         return 0;
00137 }
00138 
00139 static
00140 void
00141 fail(unsigned long num, const char *msg)
00142 {
00143         kprintf("thread %lu: Mismatch on %s\n", num, msg);
00144         kprintf("Test failed\n");
00145 
00146         lock_release(testlock);
00147 
00148         V(donesem);
00149         thread_exit();
00150 }
00151 
00152 static
00153 void
00154 locktestthread(void *junk, unsigned long num)
00155 {
00156         int i;
00157         (void)junk;
00158 
00159         for (i=0; i<NLOCKLOOPS; i++) {
00160                 lock_acquire(testlock);
00161                 testval1 = num;
00162                 testval2 = num*num;
00163                 testval3 = num%3;
00164 
00165                 if (testval2 != testval1*testval1) {
00166                         fail(num, "testval2/testval1");
00167                 }
00168 
00169                 if (testval2%3 != (testval3*testval3)%3) {
00170                         fail(num, "testval2/testval3");
00171                 }
00172 
00173                 if (testval3 != testval1%3) {
00174                         fail(num, "testval3/testval1");
00175                 }
00176 
00177                 if (testval1 != num) {
00178                         fail(num, "testval1/num");
00179                 }
00180 
00181                 if (testval2 != num*num) {
00182                         fail(num, "testval2/num");
00183                 }
00184 
00185                 if (testval3 != num%3) {
00186                         fail(num, "testval3/num");
00187                 }
00188 
00189                 lock_release(testlock);
00190         }
00191         V(donesem);
00192 }
00193 
00194 
00195 int
00196 locktest(int nargs, char **args)
00197 {
00198         int i, result;
00199 
00200         (void)nargs;
00201         (void)args;
00202 
00203         inititems();
00204         kprintf("Starting lock test...\n");
00205 
00206         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00207                 result = thread_fork("synchtest", locktestthread, NULL, i,
00208                                      NULL);
00209                 if (result) {
00210                         panic("locktest: thread_fork failed: %s\n",
00211                               strerror(result));
00212                 }
00213         }
00214         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00215                 P(donesem);
00216         }
00217 
00218         kprintf("Lock test done.\n");
00219 
00220         return 0;
00221 }
00222 
00223 static
00224 void
00225 cvtestthread(void *junk, unsigned long num)
00226 {
00227         int i;
00228         volatile int j;
00229         time_t secs1, secs2;
00230         uint32_t nsecs1, nsecs2;
00231 
00232         (void)junk;
00233 
00234         for (i=0; i<NCVLOOPS; i++) {
00235                 lock_acquire(testlock);
00236                 while (testval1 != num) {
00237                         gettime(&secs1, &nsecs1);
00238                         cv_wait(testcv, testlock);
00239                         gettime(&secs2, &nsecs2);
00240 
00241                         if (nsecs2 < nsecs1) {
00242                                 secs2--;
00243                                 nsecs2 += 1000000000;
00244                         }
00245                         
00246                         nsecs2 -= nsecs1;
00247                         secs2 -= secs1;
00248 
00249                         /* Require at least 2000 cpu cycles (we're 25mhz) */
00250                         if (secs2==0 && nsecs2 < 40*2000) {
00251                                 kprintf("cv_wait took only %u ns\n", nsecs2);
00252                                 kprintf("That's too fast... you must be "
00253                                         "busy-looping\n");
00254                                 V(donesem);
00255                                 thread_exit();
00256                         }
00257 
00258                 }
00259                 kprintf("Thread %lu\n", num);
00260                 testval1 = (testval1 + NTHREADS - 1)%NTHREADS;
00261 
00262                 /*
00263                  * loop a little while to make sure we can measure the
00264                  * time waiting on the cv.
00265                  */
00266                 for (j=0; j<3000; j++);
00267 
00268                 cv_broadcast(testcv, testlock);
00269                 lock_release(testlock);
00270         }
00271         V(donesem);
00272 }
00273 
00274 int
00275 cvtest(int nargs, char **args)
00276 {
00277 
00278         int i, result;
00279 
00280         (void)nargs;
00281         (void)args;
00282 
00283         inititems();
00284         kprintf("Starting CV test...\n");
00285         kprintf("Threads should print out in reverse order.\n");
00286 
00287         testval1 = NTHREADS-1;
00288 
00289         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00290                 result = thread_fork("synchtest", cvtestthread, NULL, i,
00291                                       NULL);
00292                 if (result) {
00293                         panic("cvtest: thread_fork failed: %s\n",
00294                               strerror(result));
00295                 }
00296         }
00297         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00298                 P(donesem);
00299         }
00300 
00301         kprintf("CV test done\n");
00302 
00303         return 0;
00304 }
